def: v.t. To mark (a document, &c.) with the letter ṣ, implying that it has been examined, or checked, or approved, &c.; to check (an account); to approve, authenticate, grant, sanction.
more: called صادمـﮩمله and صادغيرمنقوطه the 14th letter of the Arabic alphabet, and 20th in Hindustāni, does not properly occur in the Persian language. In abjad , q. v. it stands for 90. As an abbreviation (ص) it is used for صحيح q. v. When Persian words containing che are introduced into Arabic, the چ is changed into ص as صين for چين China. suād k. , v. a. 1. To approve; sign; signify the correctness of; check; make a mark. 2. To grant; sanction.

the fourteenth letter of the Arabic alphabet, does not properly occur in the Persian language; but, in a few words it has, in modern times, been substituted for the original س to distinguish them from others of a different signification; as, in صد, originally سد One hundred; and in شصت originally شست Sixty; to prevent their being confounded with سد A wall, and شست An aim. It has no corresponding letter in the Sanskrit alphabet. In Persia and Hindūstān, its pronunciation is hardly distinguishable from that of س; but, among the Arabs, its sound is stronger than the last and with a mixture of w. In the notation of numbers by the letters of the Arabic alphabet it represents 90. As an abbreviation it is used for صفر the name of the second month in the Arabian calendar. It is one of the radical letters. Some Arabic words are written indifferently with ص and س; as صراط, or سراط A road. Sometime…

def: adv. Always, perpetually, continually, ever
more: adv. S. सदा, सर्वदा Pālī, sabbadā . Ever ( āṭh pahar , 2). 1. Sadā rahe nām Allāh kā! God's name endure for aye! 2. About his wife's neck twined her husband's arm not e'er hath stayed, It hath gone down and veered and passed as of the tree the shade. 3. Ek din soī pīū binā, badan raho murjhāë, Sadā duhāgan pīū kī kaise rain bihāē? Dohā. One night just passed without my spouse hath withered up my frame, How hardly pass the nights of those who wives are but in name! 4. Sadā Shiv! dūdhā piv! (Cooing of a dove). Praise be to eternal Shiv! let me drink milk and live!

def: v.n. (fr. sādhnā, q.v.), s.f. Accomplishment, performance, fulfilment; — s.m. A present (of clothes, sweetmeats, &c.), sent by the parents to a daughter in the seventh month of pregnancy or just before her confinement.
adj. & s.m. Virtuous, good, &c. ( = sādhū, q.v.)
pl. (of ṣad, q.v.), collec. n. Hundreds (of times, &c.); many, a great many; — by hundreds; (cf. saikṛoṅ, and hazārhā).

adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Effective, accomplishing, completing, performing; helping; effecting by magic, magical; adept, skilful; holy; — an accomplisher, a practiser, a skilful person, an adept; a holy man, a devotee; — a determiner, proof, ground, justification, conclusive argument (for, -kā).
more: 1. adj. Holy, devotee. 2. s. m. A practiser.

def: s.m. The act of accomplishing, effecting, performing; settling, fixing, determining, regulating; accomplishment, completion, performance, execution, observance, fulfilment; practice; attainment (of an object), success; means of accomplishing, or effecting, or obtaining, means of success, means of subsistence; means or expedient; efficient cause, source, cause; instrument, agent; substance, matter, materials, ingredient; substantiation, proof, demonstration; accomplishing by magic or incantations, magic; propitiating, worshipping; good works, penance; observance of moral and ceremonial duties; — result; resource; wealth; profit, advantage
more: n. f. S. साध् to accomplish. 1. Performance; execution; observance. 2. Practice ( istemāl ). 3. Worship; devotion. 4. Settling; fixing; regulation; rectification.
subs: adj. To be accomplished or effected, to be done, feasible; improveable, rectifiable, &c. (see sādhan and sādhnā).

def: adj. Possessing property or riches, wealthy, rich, opulent.

ety: caus. of sadhnā, q.v.; and cf. sādhnā
def: v.t. To teach, train (an animal, &c.); to tame, familiarize, domesticate; to manage; to correct, discipline, improve; to make; to perform; to discharge.
more: v. a. S. शुध् to correct. 1. To perform; discharge. 2. To train or tame animals. Muddat meṅ ab is bachche ko hai ham ne sadhāyā, Laṛne ke sivā nāch bhī is ko hai sikhāyā . Nazīr.
see سادهن) v. a. 1. To train animals. 2. To perform.

def: s.f. Accomplishment, completion, performance, fulfilment; observance; means, contrivance, expedient; regulation; the act of familiarizing by habitude, practice; solicitation, entreaty; propitiation, adoration, worship.

adj. = sa-dhan, q.v. s.m. The act of accomplishing, effecting, performing; settling, fixing, determining, regulating; accomplishment, completion, performance, execution, observance, fulfilment; practice; attainment (of an object), success; means of accomplishing, or effecting, or obtaining, means of success, means of subsistence; means or expedient; efficient cause, source, cause; instrument, agent; substance, matter, materials, ingredient; substantiation, proof, demonstration; accomplishing by magic or incantations, magic; propitiating, worshipping; good works, penance; observance of moral and ceremonial duties; — result; resource; wealth; profit, advantage
subs: adj. To be accomplished or effected, to be done, feasible; improveable, rectifiable, &c. (see sādhan and sādhnā).

def: adj. To be accomplished or effected, feasible; to be proved.

r: sadhāʼo, sadhā, nā, āʼo, āʼū, sadhāʼū
def: s.m. Training, management, discipline, correction, improvement.

adj. & adv. Belonging or applicable to many, common, general, universal, joint; generic; public; well-known; usual, ordinary, middling, common-place, trite, so-so; plain, simple; easy; casual; — commonly, generally; ordinarily, usually; by the way, casually; inadvertently; without difficulty, easily; softly, gently
more: adj. 1. Common; everyday; common place. 2. Middling; fair; so so; usual; ordinary. 3. Simple ( sādā 2). 4. Easy ( āsān , 1). 5. Casual ( ittefāqī ). sādhāran adhikār , ( Law ) A common right. sādhāran āṅk , ( Gram .) The indefinite article. sādhāran jan rāj , n. m. A republic. sādhāran dharm , n. m. A universal duty; a law common to all castes. sādhāran rītī . A general rule or practice. sādhāran gun , ( Phys .) General property. sādhāran nām-bāchak . ( Gram .) A common noun. sādhāran , adv. 1. Commonly; usually; ordinarily. 2. Casually; by the way ( ittifāq se ). Maiṅ to sādhāran chalā āyā thā . 3. Without thought or consideration; innocently. Maiṅ ne to sādhāran kah dīyā thā . 4. Without difficulty ( āsānī se ). 5. Gently; softly; mildly.
subs: s.m. Common or universal duty, duty or law binding on all castes and orders alike
adj. & s.m. Plain-featured, plain, ordinary; — plain features; a person of plain features
s.f. A common woman, a harlot
wety: From Sanskrit साधारण (sādhāraṇa).

def: s.m. = sva-dharm, q.v.
more: s. m. Own duty: see سودهرم swa-dharma.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Lawful; — having similar duties, of the same sect or caste, observing the same customs or laws ( = sa-dharmī).
adj. Having similar duties, &c. (i.q. sa-dharm, & sa-dharmī, qq.v.); similar, like, analogous.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Observing the same customs or laws, performing like duties, of the same caste ( = sa-dharm); — a caste-fellow, &c.
s.f. A wife wedded according to the proper ritual, a lawful or virtuous wife.
s.f. A woman having a husband, a wife whose husband is living; a happy wife.

ety: sadhā(nā)+āʼū = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं; and Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: s.m.f. To be trained or taught; docile, manageable; improveable; — maker, former; trainer; manager; improver.
s.m. A name of Agni or fire.

part. Completed, finished, achieved, effected, accomplished; succeeded; habituated; settled, fulfilled; substantiated, proved; made good, discharged (as a debt); fined, amerced, punished by fine, awarded.

ety: sādh˚ = Prk. साध(इ), or साधे(इ) = S. साधय(ति), rt. साध्
def: v.t. To accomplish, effect, perform; to observe, keep; to practise, to use; to prepare, make, shape, fashion, mould; to familiarize gradually (to any habit), to habituate, accustom; to teach; train, discipline; to learn, to acquire; to show, to prove, demonstrate, substantiate; to settle, fix, determine; to regulate, order, arrange; to correct, rectify, improve, ameliorate; to modulate; to steady, balance, poise; to point (a gun); to conciliate, pacify; to propitiate, adore, worship; to hold or stop (the breath)
more: v. a. 1. To perform; accomplish. Ekahi sādhe, sab sadhe; sab sādhe sab jāë, Jo gah seve mūl ko phūle phale aghāë . Prov. But one thing do, all will come true, At all things strain, all will prove vain, Who serves the root will eat the fruit. 2. To practice; exercise; train; discipline. 3. To habituate or accustom oneself to. 4. To keep; maintain. Achchhī chup sādhī hai . 5. To regulate; correct; rectify. 6. To set; place; fix; steady; balance; poise. Naṭ badan ko khūb sādhte haiṅ . 7. To mould; form; figure. Nā raṅgī raṅgrez kī, nā sādhe kumhār. Dūlhan aisī ras-bharī gol gāt kī nār . Riddle. Ans. Nārangī . Orange. Nor hue from dyer got, nor fashioned by the potter she, A bride most luseious with a shapely rounded form you see. sādh'nī , n. f. A square, (carpenters, masons, etc.).

1. v. a. To familiarize gradually to any habit, to teach, to learn, habituate, accustom, to use, practise, regulate, to pacify, to correct, rectify, settle, fix or determine, to accomplish. 2. s. f. (1) The act of familiarizing by habitude, practice. (2) Accomplishment.
subs: v.t. (intens.) To prove, test, examine the correctness (of).

def: v.n. To be trained, be taught, be disciplined; to be familiarized; to be got ready, be finished, be made, be fashioned, &c. (see sādhnā)
more: v. n. See sādhnā , v. a. Kitne meṅ kām sadh jāëgā?
(intrans. of سادهنا q. v.) v. n. To be fully instructed or disciplined.

def: s.f. A workman's plummet or level; a mason's or carpenter's rule or square; — anything that improves or sets off; metal border or edging round the collet of a ring, &c.

adj. & s.m. (f. -vī), Perfect, excellent, good, eminent; virtuous, honourable, righteous, pious, holy, pure, benevolent; ingenuous, honest, guileless, simple, innocent; fit, proper, right; — a good or honest man, a virtuous or holy man, a saint, sage, devotee, ascetic; a kind of Hindū mendicant, one of a class of monotheistical Hindūs who profess moral and personal purity; — a merchant, a money-lender, usurer
more: adj. S. साधु 1. Pious; righteous; holy. 2. Simple; innocent. sādhū, sādh , n. m. 1. One who leads a religious life; an ascetic; a saint. 2. A sect of monotheistical Hindus who profess moral and personal purity. 1. Sādhū kahiye sūp ko, pāyā pheṅke halor, Ochhī kahiye chālnī, bhūsī rākhe baṭor . Kabīr. The winnowing fan a saint fit call who throws off all the chaff, The sieve a base one call, for it doth garner up the draff. 2. Sādh bhaë to kyā huā, gat mat jāneṅ nāhëṅ, Tulsī! peṭ ke kārne sādh bhaë jag māṅheṅ . What if you be a devotee without religion true! Tulsī! to fill their stomachs devotees are not a few. 3. Sādh chale baikunṭh ko baiṭh pālkī māṅh, Raste meṅ se āë phir, bhāṅg, tamākū nāṅh . Dohā. A saint in a sedan his journey heavenward went, But turned back on the road, his bhang and baccy spent. 4. Sādhū bachche bahute jhūṭhe, thoṛe sachche . Rus. 'Mongst holy men most liars are,…
1. adj. Pious, virtuous, good, honest. 2. s. m. A merchant. 3. A kind of mendicant.
subs: s.m. A beautiful flower; the shrub Hibiscus mutabilis
s.m. A large gathering of Hindū ascetics
s.m. One who ministers to the wants of a Hindū ascetic
vulg. sādhū-sīl, adj. Well-disposed, benign, benevolent; virtuously inclined, virtuous, righteous
s.f. The majesty or excellence of Hindū ascetics
vulg. sādhū-br̤iksh, s.m. A good tree; the Kadamb-tree, Nauclea cadamba.

def: s.f. = S سادهتو साधुत्व sādhutva, s.m. Goodness, excellence; honesty, correctness, righteousness, piety, virtue.
s.m. = S سادهتا साधुता sādhutā, s.f. Goodness, excellence; honesty, correctness, righteousness, piety, virtue.
v.t. To cause to be taught, trained, or disciplined, &c. (see sadhānā, and sadhnā).
s.f. A chaste woman, a virtuous wife.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit साध्वी (sādhvī).

ety: S. साध्यास्, m. pl. of sādhya
def: s.m. pl. lit. 'The perfect or pure ones'; a class of celestial beings belonging to the Gaṇa-devatā (syn. sādhyā-gaṇ; — see gaṇ).
s.f. Daughter of Daksha, and wife of Dharma (regarded as the mother of the Sādhyas).

adj. & s.m. To be accomplished or effected; to be formed; practicable, feasible, attainable, easy; to be proved or demonstrated; to be substantiated; — accomplishment, perfection; a thing that has to be accomplished or to be established by proof; a proposition (of Euclid); — name of a certain class of inferior deities (whose number is variously given as twelve and seventeen); — name of the twenty-second of the astronomical periods called Yogas
subs: adj. Possessed of the power of effecting a thing, equal to, adequate to, sufficient
adj. To be accomplished by application or energy
s.m. pl. Name of a class of celestial beings ( = sādhya, and sādhyā, qq.v.).

def: s.m. Name of the celebrated poet and moralist of Shīrāz, author of the Gulistān.

ety: ṣad, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. A century; centenary
more: n. f. A century; centenary. fī sadī ; H. saiṅkṛā . Per cent.
ex: اِکِّیسوِیں صَدی
ikkīsvī̃ sadī
twenty-first century

کُچھ بات ہَے کِہ ہَسْتی مِٹْتی نَہِیں ہَماری
صَدِیوں رَہَا ہَے دُشْمَن دَورِ زَماں ہَمارا
kuch bāt hai ki hastī miṭtī nahī̃ hamārī
sadiyõ rahā hai duśman daur-i zamā̃ hamārā

There is something about our existence for it doesn't get wiped
Even though, for centuries, the time-cycle has been our enemy.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صدی (sadī, “a hundred”).

def: s.f. corr. of شادي shādī, q.v. s.f. Pleasure, delight, joy, rejoicing, festivity; marriage; a wedding; a marriage-feast
subs: To rejoice; to make a feast, to feast; to perform a religious ceremony previous to entering a new house; — to marry
(-kī), To get (a boy, or girl) married (to, -se)
(-kī), To marry (a child, to); — to dispose (of, — a horse, &c.)
s.f. Death from joy; dying easily, an easy death

ety: act. part. of صدق 'to speak or utter truth,' &c.
def: adj. True (in respect of speech, &c.), veracious; (a man) of his word; sincere; faithful, just: exact
more: adj. صدق told the truth. 1. True; faithful ( sachchā , 1). 2. Applicable; exact ( ṭhīk , 4, 6). Tum par voh hī masal sādiq ātī hai. sādiq-ul-etiqād . Firm in one's faith. sādiq-ul-qaul , True to one's word; truthful. sādiq ānā , v. n. 1. To come true; to be verified. 2. To become; fit; suit. ba-taur-i-māqūl sādiq ānā , G. G. To reasonably answer. dost-i-sādiq, yār-i-sādiq , A true friend.
(part. act. of صدق Heb. To be, or be esteemed, just) adj. True, just, sincere. صادق القول ṣādiḳu-l-ḳaul, Speaking truth.
subs: (-par), To come or prove true (in the case of), to be verified (in); to be applicable (to), to apply (to); — to suit, fit, become
adj. True to (one's) faith, firm in (one's) belief
adj. True of speech, truthful, veracious; true to (one's) word.

def: adj. & s.m. Just, true, sincere, faithful; — a true or sincere friend.
more: adj. Just, true; a sincere friend.

def: Sadiqabad (a city in Rahim Yar Khan district, Punjab, Pakistan)
wety: From صادِق (sādiq, “Sadeq Mohammad Khan V”) + آباد (ābād).

def: s.f. Truthfulness, truth, sincerity, &c. ( = ṣidq).

ety: act. part. of صدر 'to issue, proceed, &c.
def: adj. Issuing, going forth, proceeding, emanating (from, -se); issued, passed; produced; derived; happened
more: p. p. صدر issued. 1. Proceeding; going forth; issning. 2. Issued; passed. sādir yā sudūr k. , v. a. To issue; pass (an order); enact. sādir-kunindah . One who issues (an order). sādir yā sudūr h. , v. n. 1. To be issued or enacted. 2. To happen; befal. 3. To be committed or perpetrated. sudūr-i-hukm . Passing of an order or verdict.
(part. act. of صدر) adj. Proceeding, produced, derived, happened, arrived, passed, issued. صادرهونا ṣādir honā, To proceed, to be produced, derived, emerged, issued, &c.
subs: v.t. To issue, put forth, pass (an order, &c.)

v.n. To be issued, be passed (as an order, &c.): to issue, proceed, emanate (from, -se); to be produced, be derived (from); to be committed or perpetrated (by, — as a fault); to occur, happen, befall.

def: adj. Sixth.
adv. Sixthly, in the sixth place.
direct plural of صَدی (sadī)

ety: sādh(nā) + Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य + ता + इका
def: s.f. The female of the little bird called Amadavat (syn. ćittī; muniyā); — a kind of faqīr or religious mendicant.
more: n. f. The female Amadavat ( chiṭṭī ).

ety: for A. سعدية, fr. saʻdī, fr. saʻd, q.v.
def: s.f. Auspiciousness, luckiness, felicity.
oblique plural of صَدی (sadī)

ety: for A. صدقة ṣadaqat, v.n. fr. صدق; see ṣidq
def: s.m. An alms; a gift, donation; a propitiatory offering, a sacrifice (to avert misfortune, or sickness, &c.); a person or thing devoted; — devotion (to); favour, grace, kindness
more: n. m. 1. Alms dedicated to pious uses. 2. (Wom. sadqa sillā ) Propitiatory offerings to avert sickness ( utārā , 2). 3. Gift; donation. Ṭīm ṭām kī pagṛi bāṅdhī, woh bhī sadqa jorū kā . Song. He sports a turban grand, his wife the cost must stand. 4. Favor; grace; kindness. Yeh hamārā hī sadqah hai. sadqā utārnā k., yā denā , v. a. See utārā utārnā. sadqe jānā, yā vārī jānā yā h. , v. n. See balihārī jānā . Bībī kahtī hai, 'Mīyāṅ tere maiṅ sadqe jāūṅ!' Sās bolī 'kahīṅ mat jā, tere sadqe jāūṅ!' Khāla kahtī hai ki 'kuchh khā, tere sadqe jāūṅ!' Sāli kahtī hai kī, 'bhaiyā, tere sadqe jāūṅ!' Jo khushāmad kare khalq us se sadā rāzī hai! Nazīr . The wife then says 'My lord, my life I'll give for you!' Her mother says 'Oh go not, here's my life for you!' The aunt says 'Eat now, do, my life I'd give for you!' The sister-in-law says 'Brother, here's my life for you!' Who fawn and flatter with them …

(prop. ṣadaḳa, Heb. ) s. m. 1. Alms. 2. Propitiatory offerings, sacrifice. صدقي جانا يا هونا ṣadḳe-jānā or -honā, To become a sacrifice for the welfare, &c. of another. صدقي کرنا ṣadḳe karnā, To sacrifice for the welfare, &c. of another.
subs: v.t. To draw off or away (an evil spirit) by a sacrifice (or by walking round the person, repeating an incantation, &c.)
(-par), To become, or to offer oneself, as a sacrifice for the welfare, &c. (of another); to move round (a person) by way of exhibiting devotion, or affection, or reverence (for him or her)
v.t. To sacrifice (a thing, or oneself, for, -par, the welfare of another); to devote oneself (for, -par)
My life is devoted to you; my life for you

ety: for A. صدمة, n. of un. fr. ṣadmuṉ, inf. n. of صدم 'to dash or knock against'
def: s.m. A shock, collision; a blow, contusion, injury; a stroke of fortune, adversity, misfortune, calamity, accident
more: n. m. صدم knocked against. A blow ( choṭ , 1, 2, & دهکا 1, 3, 4). sadmā uṭhānā , v. a. See dhakkā lagnā. sadmā pahoṅchānā , v. a. To give a blow; to hurt; injure ( taklīf d .). sadmā pahoṅchānā gusse meṅ . An assault in hot blood. sadma-i-jāṅ-kāh , 1. A hard blow. 2. A heavy affliction. sadma-i-jismānī . Personal violence; bodily hurt. sadme se , adv. With great force. sadma-i-azīm . A severe blow; severe bodily injury.
(from صدم To impinge, to dash) s. m. A blow, a stroke of fortune, shock, adversity, accident, calamity, collision, an adventure.
subs: (-kā), To experience a shock (of); to suffer a blow, &c.
v.t. To cause (one) to receive a shock or blow, &c.; to give (one) a blow; to bring injury on (one), to hurt, injure
s.m. Bodily hurt or injury, personal violence
adv. With a shock; with vehemence or violence, with great force.

ety: sad˚ = Prk. संद(इ) = S. स्यन्द(ते), rt. स्यन्द्
def: v.n. To leak (as a ship, &c.); — to settle down, to sink.
v. a. (dakh.) See سادهنا sādhnā.
adj. Having faults, faulty, defective; improper, wrong; objectionable; sinful, guilty.
adj. = sa-dosh, q.v. adj. Having faults, faulty, defective; improper, wrong; objectionable; sinful, guilty.
Wom. adj. S. सदा + उत्तमा. ( Ironic .) Old and experienced. (Said to a young woman who talks like her elders).
adv. At the top; above; before (used in comp., e.g. mufaṣṣila-ě-ṣadar, 'detailed above, or before-detailed')
more: adj. صدر facing, upper. Principal; capital (اَعلي). sadr, sadar , n. m. 1. ( Anat .) The chest ( chhātī , 1). 2. The head man; chief; principal. Sadar har jā ki nashīnad sadar ast . P. Prov. The highest place is where the chief sits. 3. The upper end of a table; the highest seat. 4. The seat of Government; the presidency, as opposed to the provinces or the Mufassil; the head quarters of a district. 5. A military cantonment. sadar amīn , n. m. 1. A head amīn , q. v. 2. A subordinate judge lower than sadr- āla , q. v. The office has been abolished. sadar bāzār , n. m. The chief bazar in a military cantonment. sadar borḍ , n. m. The highest revenue court; the Board of Revenue. sadar panchāyat . A supreme panchāyat , q. v. sadar jama yā māl-guzārī , n. f. The sum total of revenue payable to Government direct, exclusive of cesses, etc. sadar dīvān, dīvān-i-ālā , n. m. A chancellor. sadar dīvā…

(from صدر To proceed, to come forth, to become publick, to be revealed: Ch. To arrange, to regulate) s. f. 1. The breast, the foremost or highest part of any thing, the first place or seat in an assembly. 2. Exaltation, preeminence. 3. Chief, supreme. 4. Government. صدرصدور ṣadr-i-ṣudūr, The chief judge, chancellor. صدرنشين ṣadr-nishīn, A president, one sitting in the first seat.
subs: s.m. Head or chief amīn, q.v.; — a subordinate judge (lower than ṣadr-aʻlạ̄; — the office has been abolished)
s.m. The chief bazār in a military cantonment
s.m. The Board of Revenue, the highest revenue court
s.f. The High Court of Justice for civil suits (in India)
s.m. The chief revenue office in a district; the collector's office; — the highest authority or head of an office
or ṣadr-ě-ṣudūr, or ṣadr-ṣudūr, s.m. Chief judge; chancellor; (in India), the principal Sadr-amīn (now called a subordinate judge)
s.f. The Supreme Court of Justice
s.m. The head-man or representative of a joint-tenancy village who engages for and pays the revenue due from the community to the government
s.f. The sum-total of revenue payable to government direct (exclusive of cesses, &c.)
s.m. 'Sitter in the chief seat'; a president, chairman.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَدْر (sadr), from Arabic صَدْر (ṣadr).

def: s.m. A kind of song.
more: s. m. A kind of song.

def: president, head of state
wety: Ezafe compound of صدر (sadar, “chief, head”) + مملکت (mumlikat, “dominion”), literally “chief or head of the dominion”.

def: s.f. A vest, waistcoat; a jacket (cf. ćapkan).
adj. Like, resembling, similar; conformable, fit, proper, right, suitable; worthy; — postpn. After the similitude (of, -ke), after the manner (of), conformably (to).
s.f. = S سدرشتو सदृशत्व sa-dr̤iśatva, s.m. Likeness, similarity, sameness.
s.m. = S سدرشتا सदृशता sa-dr̤iśatā, s.f. Likeness, similarity, sameness.
s.m.(f.?), Likeness, resemblance, similarity.
oblique plural of صَدْر (sadr)
vocative plural of صَدْر (sadr)
adv. To-day, this very day; now, instantly, at the moment, at this (or that) very time; forthwith, immediately, at once, just now; on a sudden.
alternative form of سایَہ دار (sāya dār)
s.f. Rubbing the forehead (on the ground in adoration).

n. m. A sun-shade; canopy; the roof or fly of a tent; a thatched roof. sāyah-parvardah , adj. lit . brought up under one's shadow. 1. A protegé . 2. A slave. sāyah paṛ'nā , v. n. 1. To come under one's influence. 2. To imitate another. Tum par bhī bhāī kā sāyā paṛ gayā. sāyah h., yā ho jānā, sāye meṅ ānā , v. n. To be possessed by an evil spirit. sāe-dār , adj. Shady. sāë tale ānā , v. n. To come under one's protection.

ety: for A. صاف ṣāfiṉ, act. part. of صَفَا, rt. صفو 'to be clean or pure,' &c.
def: adj. Pure, clean, clear; calm, serene; open, unclouded, bright (as the sky); open, sincere, candid, frank; simple, innocent; plain, simple, easy of comprehension, evident; distinct, perspicuous (a speech, &c.); correct, true, exact, precise (as an account, &c.); downright, decided, point-blank (as a refusal or denial, &c.); out and out, thorough, complete, entire; smooth, flat, level (as a surface or plain, &c.); — adv. Clearly; plainly; distinctly; openly, candidly, without reserve or guile; clean out, outright; decidedly, flatly; thoroughly, entirely
more: adj. صفو was clear. 1. Clear; fair; clean ( pāk , 3). Kyā sāf jagah hai! 2. Cleaned out; swept. Dastoṅ se peṭ sāf ho gayā . 3. Unsoiled; unspotted ( pāk , 1). Sāf kapṛā . 4. Unadulterated ( pāk , 4). 5. Clear; transparent; bright. 6. Unruffled; calm; smooth. 7. Legible; clear. Sāf likhā huā hai . 8. Perspicuous; lucid. 9. Apparent ( āshkārā ). Sāf bāt hai! 10. Open; frank; candid Sāf dil . 11. Distinct; express; direct. 1. Sāf bayān . 2. Sāf talaffuz . 12. Flat; level; even ( chauras ). Kyā safā maidān hai! 13. Regular; methodical ( ṭhīk , 11). 14. True ( ṭhīk , 2). 15. Indisputable ( ṭhīk , 2). Sāf hisāb hai . 16. ( do ṭūk ) Decided; peremptory ( akhīr , 3). 17. Untouched; unmolested. 18. Well-practised; expert; ready. 19. Lat. integer , ( Bot .) Entire. sāf inkār k., sāf mukarnā , v. a. To deny or decline outright. sāf bolnā , v. n. To utter or speak distinctly. sāf bayān k., sāf kahnā…
adv. 1. Distinctly; clearly; plainly; expressly; in plain terms. 2. Frankly; openly. 3. Without reserve or guile. 4. Outright; entirely; thoroughly. sāf sāf , adv. Emphatic of sāf , adv.
(contract. of صافي part. act. of صفو Heb. To be clean or clear) adj. Clean, clear, pure, candid, innocent, downright, flat. صاف دل ṣāf-dil, adj. Of a pure heart, candid, frank.
subs: adv. Clearly, openly, clean out; without difficulty; at once; exactly, precisely, &c. (see ṣāf, of which these are emphat. forms)
v.n. To get clean off; to be liberated or acquitted; to escape unscathed
adj. Simple-hearted, pure-hearted, guileless, open-hearted, candid, frank
v.n. To be pure, or clean, &c.; to fast, to starve

v.t. To clean, clear, cleanse, purify; to clarify; to wash, make white; to rinse; to practise (the hand); to make a fair copy of; to clear (a jungle, &c.), cut down trees; — ṣāf kar-jānā (-ko), To make a clean sweep (of), to ransack; to finish, eat up; — ṣāf kar-denā (-ko), To clean away, clear away, &c.
v.n. To get clean away; to get off scot-free; to make off with all speed
v.n. To be cleaned, &c.; to be cleared (as a road or jungle); to be settled (as a dispute or difference); to be removed (as an obstacle).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَاف (sāf), from Arabic صَافٍ (ṣāfin).

ety: inf. n. of صفا, rt. صفو 'to be clear,' &c.
def: s.f. Clearness, transparency; polish, brightness; cleanness, purity; serenity (of life or mind); freedom from trouble; comfort; content; complacency; happiness, joy, pleasure, recreation; — name of a mountain near Mecca; — adj. Pure, clean, &c. ( = ṣāf, q.v.)

subs: adj. 'Smacksmooth,' without a trace or vestige
adj. Endowed with integrity, sincere; purified, pure; purged
v.t. To make smack-smooth, to lick (a platter) perfectly clean; to make a clean shave of, to shave clean.

s.m. Account of a journey, travels
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سفر (safar), from Arabic سَفَر (safar). Cognate of English safari.

def: s.m. The second month of the Arabian calendar (on the 20th day of this month is held the festival of Sar-o-tan, in commemoration of the junction of the head and body of Husain: the 28th and the last days of this month are reckoned unfortunate; the former on account of Hasan's being poisoned by his wife; the latter because destructive vegetables are supposed to be produced on that day)
more: n. m. The 2nd month of the Mah. year.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Own, full, related, born of the same parents; akin, kin; — a blood relation, relative, kin
more: adj. S. स्वकीय Own; full; whole blood. Sagā bhāī . Own brother. sagā , n. m. sagī , n. f. 1. A blood relation. 1. Suārath ke sabhī sage, bin suārath koū nāṅhe, Jaise panchhī saras taru, niras bhaë ur jāṅhen . Braṅd-satsai. To self allied all are, from selfishness no one is free, As birds alight on fresh green trees, but shun the sapless tree. 2. Yeh kisī kā bhī sagā nahīṅ hai . Prov. Saying. has kinship with none. (He keeps faith with none). He 2. Fae . for sag a dog. Yeh tumhāre sagon meṅ hai? Is he one of your dogs? sagā'rat , n. f. Blood relationship; kin. sagā-sodrā , Wom.; Sant. mit lāi: ren ( lit . of one womb); n. m. sagī-sodrī , n. f. S. सढ with उदर womb. 1. One's own brother. 2. Of the same family. sage sambandhī . Relations.
1. adj. Related (of the same parents), full, own; as, سگا بهاٴي sagā bhāʼī, Own brother. 2. A relative, akin. 3. s. m. Kin. 4. (dakh.) Friend, intimate.
subs: (sodrā = S. saha+udaras), s.m. Own brother; kinsman, relation; — adj. Co-uterine; of the same family

ety: sagā, q.v. + āwat = Prk. अवत्ता = S. क+वार्त्ता; — and āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Relationship by the same parents, consanguinity, family relation, kin; — betrothing, betrothal (syn. maṅgnī); — second marriage (of a woman of low tribe)
more: n. f. 1. Betrothal. 1. Rāṅḍā gayā sagāī ko, āp ko lāë yā bhāī ko? Prov. The widower's gone to fetch a wife. A wife for self, or for his brother must he get.? (One's own work is first). 2. E. A second marriage among the lower classes. 3. Relationship; kin. 2. Muī māī, ṭūṭī sagāī . Prov. The mother dead, all kinship dead. sagāī k .; Sant. horo: chikhnā , v. a. To betroth; contract a marriage; to affiance. 1. Chhorī kī sagāī kar dūṅ, tau nichaṅt ho jāūṅ . Hin. Wom. 2. Kaljug ke yeh log, yār, jisko kahte bhaujāī, Usī ke saṅg meṅ bhog karte haiṅ kar-ke sagāī . Song. Lāonī . It is the iron age, with elder sister-in-law, bhaujāī Men loose connexions make, my friend, by custom called sagāī .
subs: (-kī), To contract a marriage, to betroth, affiance.

def: s.m. A prognostic, an omen, an augury; — a present of money
more: n. m. S. शकुन, P. shugūn . 1. An augury; omen. ☞ A jar full of water, a pregnant woman, etc. are good omens when setting out on a journey; while a black object, a maimed person, an empty water jar, a sneeze in front of one, etc. are bad omens. 1. Kin kī lagan, kin ko sugan . E. Prov. Nor starry influence nor omen thence. (Lose no time). 2. Parāë shugūn ke līye apnī nāk kaṭāte haiṅ . Prov. Ill omen to another shows by sacrificing his own nose. (He bites his nose to spite his face). 2. A present of money. shagun bichārnā , v. a. 1. To look for a happy conjunction of the planets, or a good omen. 2. To practise augury or astrology. Sāṅjh bhaī, surjan nahīṅ āë, āchho nīko sugan bichār! Song. Rāgnī . shugan k , v. a. To begin a work in a lucky moment. shugan lenā , v. n. To take an omen. shugūn'yā , n. m. A fortune-teller; soothsayer.
subs: To look for a good omen; to look for an auspicious conjunction of planets; to practise augury or astrology
To begin a work, &c. at an auspicious moment
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit शकुन (śakuna).
